content.full_text:
(

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 13, 1994

Mary Lou Morey
City Clerk
City of Westminster
6200 Westminster Boulevard
Westminster, CA 92663

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-202

Dear Ms. Morey:

You have requested advice regarding the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹

QUESTIONS

1. Is the Westminster Fire Fighters Association Political
Action Committee (WFFAPAC) a city, county or state committee?

2. What are your responsibilities regarding a state
political action committee which was active in a local recall?

CONCLUSION

1. WFFAPAC is a state committee based on the amended
Statement of Organization executed on August 17, 1992.

2. A state political action committee would ordinarily have
no obligation to file statements with a city clerk and a city
clerk would have no obligations in regard to the committee.

FACTS

You have indicated that the WFFAPAC has been making political
contributions to candidates for statewide office and also to
statewide ballot measure committees. The committee filed an
amendment to its Statement of Organization (Form 410) on August

_______________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

f

Morey, Mary Lou
No. A-94-202
Page 2


17, 1992, indicating its intention to be politically active at the
city, county and state levels. The last statement filed with you
by WFFAPAC was a semi-annual statement for the period ending
December 31, 1993.

ANALYSIS

Section 82027.5 provides that:

... (b) A "state general purpose committee" is a
committee to support or oppose candidates or measures
voted on in a state election, or in more than one
county.

(c) A "county general purpose committee" is a committee
to support or oppose candidates or measures voted on in
only one county, or in more than one jurisdiction within
one county.

(d) A "city general purpose committee" is a committee
to support or oppose candidates or measures voted on in
only one city.

Since WFFAPAC indicated an intention to be active at the
city, county and state levels, it is a state committee.

Section 84215 provides that:

All candidates, elected officers, committees, and
proponents of state ballot measures or the
qualifications of state ballot measures, except as
provided in subdivision (e), shall file two copies of
the campaign statements required by Section 84200 with
the clerk of the county in which they are domiciled. A
committee is domiciled at the address listed on the
campaign statement unless it is domiciled outside
California in which case its domicile shall be deemed to
be Los Angeles County for the purpose of this section.
(Emphasis added.)

A review of the most current amendment to WFFAPAC's Statement
of Organization (Form 410) and the most recently filed campaign
statement (Form 420) indicates that the committee's address is in
Riverside County. Therefore, WFFAPAC must file two copies of its
campaign statements with Riverside County.

Section 84215 further provides that:

... campaign statements shall be filed at the following
places:

(a) ... all state general purpose committees ...

(1) The original and one copy with the Secretary
of State.

Morey, Mary Lou
No. A-94-202
Page 3


(2)  Two copies with the Registrar-Recorder of Los
Angeles County.

(3)  Two copies with the Registrar of Voters of the
City and County of San Francisco.

Ordinarily, a state general purpose recipient committee would
have no obligation to file any statements with a city clerk.
However, if such a committee were to make independent expenditures
totaling $500 or more to support or oppose a city candidate, city
measure or qualification of a city measure, it would be obligated
to file independent expenditure reports in the city, as if it were
formed or existing primarily to support or oppose candidates or
measure or qualification of the measure. (Section 84203.5.) This
activity would be reported on a Form 465 and the filing officer
would place the statement in the file of the candidate or
committee which was supported or opposed. So long as a state
general purpose recipient committee is making contributions,
rather than independent expenditures, to support or oppose
candidates or committees, it has no duty to file statements with
the city filing officer.

In addition, because a state general purpose recipient
committee has no filing obligations with you, you have no
responsibilities in regard to such a committee. Your duties
extend only to entities obligated to file original statements with
you.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
[signature]
By: Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant
